ks1.objective: Gently throw a ball up so your partner can catch it through a hoop.
successCriteria: Throw is soft enough to catch
Partner moves the hoop to track the ball

lks2.objective: Develop throwing accuracy and tracking — control your throw so your partner can predict and catch through the hoop.
successCriteria: Partner reads the ball flight early

uks2.objective: Refine throwing weight and partner communication — adjust throws based on partner's positioning and react to misses with quick resets.
successCriteria: Throws adjust to partner's hoop position
Reads flight and intercepts the ball's path

equipment.item: Soft balls (tennis ball, sponge ball, balloon)

space.description: Open space — enough for pairs to spread out without throws clashing. Outdoor grass or hall.

steps: Pair up. One child holds a hoop, the other has a ball.
The thrower gently throws the ball up into the air.
Their partner moves the hoop to catch the ball through it before it lands.
After 3 attempts, swap roles so both children try both jobs.
Count how many successful 'special deliveries' each pair can make.
questions: How do you control your throw so your partner has a good chance to catch it through the hoop?
How are you moving the hoop to track the ball's path?
How do you react when it doesn't work — what helps you stay positive?

step.space: Increase or decrease the throwing distance
step.task: Allow one bounce of the ball before the catch
step.people: Two throwers, one hoop catcher — choose which ball to take

step.space: Throw from very close — almost touching the hoop holder
step.task: Hoop holder can place the hoop on the ground for a bounce-in
step.people: Coach can be the thrower for less confident pairs
